text: CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 45 DAYS: Israel-Lebanon agree to 45-day ceasefire extension after third round of US-hosted Washington talks. New expiry: ~July 1. Israeli delegation: Ron Dermer. Lebanese delegation: Simon Karam. Pentagon military security track: May 29. Next political round: June 2-3. State Dept Tommy Pigott: talks were highly productive. Hezbollah not party, continues firing in south Lebanon.
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour confirmed via State Dept spokesman Tommy Pigott. Euronews confirmed delegation names. France24 confirmed ceasefire extension announcement.

text: IRGC SEIZES FLOATING ARMORY: Honduras-flagged Hui Chan (operated by Chinese security firm Sinoguards) seized 38nm NE of Fujairah by what UKMTO confirms were unauthorized personnel — vessel directed toward Iranian waters. Marine Log identifies it as a floating armory. Iran VP Aref same day: Hormuz belongs to Iran and we will not give it up at any price. Signal: IRGC still controlling access amid ceasefire.
verificationNote: UKMTO confirmed seizure. NBC News confirmed vessel being directed toward Iranian waters. Marine Log identified floating armory type. NPR confirmed Iran VP Aref statement.

text: IDF DAY 77: 40+ Hezbollah sites struck, ~15 operatives killed, primed rocket launchers aimed at Israel destroyed. Golani Reconnaissance completed WEEKLONG Litani River raid — crossed river, destroyed 1,000+ Hezbollah infrastructure items (tunnels, depots, firing positions), dozens killed. Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan (20, Golani) killed by Hezbollah mortar fire near Litani — 6th IDF death since Lebanon ceasefire.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblogs confirmed 40+ sites, 15 killed, Litani raid details. Jerusalem Post and JNS confirmed Dagan death with name, unit, and location.

text: ISRAEL STRIKES HAMAS LEADER: 3 IAF jets dropped 13 bombs on Izz al-Din Haddad (Hamas Gaza Military Operations chief) in Rimal, Gaza City. Senior Israeli security official: initial indications Haddad was killed — unconfirmed. Defense Minister Katz immediately called a hostage family that Haddad had held. Strike signals Israel maintaining operational tempo in Gaza despite Iran ceasefire.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed 13 bombs, 3 jets, Rimal neighborhood, initial indications of kill. CNN and Jerusalem Post independently confirmed the strike.

text: TRUMP THREATENS ESCALATION: Truth Social — military decimation of Iran to be continued! Fox News interview: I am not going to be much more patient. They should make a deal. House War Powers resolution fails THIRD TIME — 212-212 tie vote (Democrat Jared Golden crossed aisle to oppose). BRICS summit New Delhi ends WITHOUT joint statement — first in BRICS history. UAE blocked Iran-sympathetic language. India forced to issue chair summary only.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Trump Truth Social post. Fox News confirmed interview quote. Democracy Now confirmed 212-212 House vote and Golden defection. WION and Al Jazeera confirmed BRICS collapse without joint statement.

text: UAE HORMUZ BYPASS: UAE announces fast-track of second West-East oil pipeline through Fujairah hub — operational 2027, nearly doubling Hormuz-bypass capacity from ~1.5M to ~3M bbl/day. Major strategic response to Hormuz closure. SAME DAY IRGC seized a vessel off Fujairah — underscoring why UAE is building the bypass. Brent 06.89/bbl (+33.9%), WTI 01.86. Gas .53/gal. Hormuz Blockade Day 74.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed UAE pipeline announcement and 2027 operational date. Bloomberg confirmed pipeline capacity details. Al Arabiya confirmed UAE acceleration decision.

text: BRICS ARAGHCHI SPEECH HIGHLIGHTS: At New Delhi BRICS FM meeting, Araghchi told assembled ministers: To virtually everyone in this room, our resistance against US bullying is not an unfamiliar battle. Many of us encounter slight variations of the same repugnant coercion. Singled out UAE for supporting Israel and US aggression. Summit ended without joint statement — UAE and others blocked Iran-sympathetic language. Sign of BRICS fracture over Iran war.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera confirmed full Araghchi BRICS speech text. Democracy Now confirmed key quote. WION confirmed summit collapse without joint statement and UAE blocking role.

text: CEASEFIRE EXPIRY WATCH: Iran-US ceasefire nominally expires May 17 — Day 79. No deal in sight. Trump returns from Beijing with no binding Iran commitments from Xi. Araghchi says ceasefire is 'very shaky.' Trump on Fox News: 'I am not going to be much more patient.' Pentagon sources tell outlets new strike package options ready for presidential authorization. Strait of Hormuz Blockade Day 75.
verificationNote: Fox News confirmed Trump patience statement. Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi 'very shaky' quote. CNBC confirmed Xi summit produced no binding Iran deal.

text: ARAGHCHI DOUBTS US SERIOUSNESS: Iranian FM stated Iran received new US messages expressing willingness to continue negotiations but Tehran has 'no trust in the Americans.' Araghchi: nuclear enrichment issue 'postponed' — not being discussed in current talks. US demands Iran ship its enriched uranium out of the country; Iran refuses. Nuclear deadlock is the core sticking point preventing any deal.
verificationNote: ANI News confirmed US message to Iran. Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi nuclear deadlock statement. WION confirmed enriched uranium issue 'postponed' framing.

text: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT OUTCOME ON IRAN: Trump says Xi offered diplomatic help to end Iran war but made no binding commitments on Hormuz reopening. Both leaders agreed Strait 'must remain open' — but Iran was not party to that statement. Xi ruled out military assistance. Trump declined to ask Xi for 'favors.' China's foreign ministry: 'no point continuing the war.' Chinese vessels permitted to sail Hormuz — suggesting IRGC selective enforcement.
verificationNote: CBS News confirmed Trump-Xi joint Hormuz statement. CNBC confirmed Xi's no-military-aid position. Al Jazeera confirmed Chinese vessels permitted passage. Fox News liveblog confirmed summit details.

text: ISRAEL-LEBANON 45-DAY CEASEFIRE EXTENSION: State Dept confirmed Israel and Lebanon agreed to 45-day ceasefire extension after 'productive and positive' Washington talks May 14-15. Next political round June 2-3. Security/military track launches Pentagon May 29. Delegation: Lebanon led by Simon Karam, Israel by Ron Dermer. Hezbollah disarmament remains unresolved — IDF still striking Hezbollah sites despite extension. Netanyahu confirmed troops remain in southern Lebanon.
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour confirmed State Dept announcement. Haaretz liveblog confirmed delegation names and June 2-3 next round. Time magazine confirmed Netanyahu troop presence statement.

text: TRUMP SOFTENS ON NUCLEAR DEMAND: On Air Force One departing Beijing, Trump signaled willingness to accept 20-year Iranian nuclear suspension (vs. permanent ban previously demanded) if it's 'real.' Also weighing lifting sanctions on Chinese companies buying Iranian oil. Congressional pushback: 52 senators + 177 House members sent letter demanding Trump reject any deal permitting enrichment. Policy shift could unlock talks — or face domestic veto.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed Trump 20-year suspension statement from Air Force One. CNBC also confirmed Chinese oil sanctions consideration. Congressional letter confirmed by multiple outlets.

text: MOJTABA KHAMENEI DEFIANCE SIGNAL: Iranian state TV broadcast statement attributed to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei: 'The only place for the United States in the Persian Gulf is at the bottom of its waters.' Still no public appearance — 75+ days since appointment. US intelligence assessed Khamenei is 'mentally sharp but isolated.' Pezeshkian claims 2.5-hour private meeting. IRGC military council reportedly blocking presidential access to Supreme Leader.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Khamenei statement via state media. CNN reported US intel assessment on May 8. Iran International reported IRGC blocking access claim. No independent verification of Khamenei's condition.
